name: Mel Tormé
abstract: Melvin Howard Tormé (September 13, 1925 – June 5, 1999), nicknamed "The Velvet Fog", was an American musician, singer, composer, arranger, drummer, actor, and author. He composed the music for "The Christmas Song" ("Chestnuts Roasting on an Open Fire") and co-wrote the lyrics with Bob Wells.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I think, didn’t Mel Torme and his partner write “The Christmas Song” in the middle of July too, sitting by a swimming pool? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, Connecticut, November 21, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: I remember in San Francisco I worked with Mel Torme, he came in so we got a band together to play for him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: my first influences were Mel Torme, Sinatra, Ella, Sarah Vaughan...
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Did you ever hear the record called Guilty that we did with them?
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Allred
about_quotes.evidence: they had a 12-piece band playing for dancing, but also backing shows and a fine place to eat. So you could go there, I mean people from town could go there and have dinner, a nice dinner and dance a little bit, see The Four Freshmen, Mel Torme, Trini Lopez, people like that, semi-names
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Allred interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: J. Leo Rayhill
about_quotes.evidence: And Mel Torme tried to keep the band going there for a while and he had some good — but his, really right from the beginning, when he had the band in the middle forties, were good arrangers.
about_quotes.doc_title: J. Leo Rayhill interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 23, 2007
